title: Inventory pollinator-monitoring, crop-production, habitat-change, and pesticide-use data sources for LMIC agricultural regions
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates pollinator-monitoring databases (GBIF, iNaturalist, regional surveys), crop-production maps (MapSPAM, FAO), satellite habitat-change products (Sentinel-2, Landsat), and pesticide-use proxies into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit LMIC coverage assessment.
inputs: IPBES pollinator assessment and referenced studies
Sentinel-2 and Landsat land-cover product documentation
GBIF and iNaturalist pollinator occurrence data availability by region
FAO pesticide-use statistics and national agricultural survey reports
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic coverage, taxonomic scope, and documented LMIC data gaps.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering geographic coverage, taxonomic scope, temporal resolution, and LMIC data gaps.

title: Map sub-national crop-pollination dependence for at least two LMIC agricultural regions using MapSPAM data
outcome: A sub-national crop-pollination-dependence map for at least two LMIC regions, documenting which crops are classified as pollinator-dependent, the source of each classification, and whether the classification was derived from temperate or tropical studies.
inputs: MapSPAM crop-production data for target regions
Administrative boundary data for sub-national aggregation
expected_artifact: validation.md update with crop-pollination classification methodology and regional applicability assessment.
validation_method: Replicator confirms MapSPAM data extraction, crop classification methodology, and pollination-dependence source documentation for each target region.
done_condition: Crop-pollination-dependence map includes methodology documentation, classification source for each crop, temperate-vs-tropical applicability assessment, and replication status.

title: Analyze satellite-derived habitat-change trends as pollinator habitat pressure proxies for target regions
outcome: A habitat-change analysis linking satellite land-cover trends to pollinator habitat pressure indicators, with explicit documentation that the habitat-to-pollinator inference chain relies on proxy relationships not validated with ground monitoring in the analyzed regions.
inputs: Sentinel-2 or Landsat land-cover change data for target regions
Published studies on landscape simplification and pollinator abundance
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with habitat-change methodology and proxy-validation documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms satellite data extraction, land-cover trend methodology, and proxy-relationship documentation with explicit limitations.
done_condition: Habitat-change analysis includes methodology, trend results, explicit proxy-validation limitations, and documentation of the absence of ground-truth pollinator monitoring in analyzed regions.

title: Red-team pollinator-decline overstatement and crop-loss attribution risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how proxy-based pollination-service risk maps could overstate pollinator decline, attribute crop-production changes to pollinator loss without evidence, or be used to justify interventions that undermine farmer confidence in natural pollination services.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every pollination-risk claim has documented proxy limitations, absence of direct pollinator monitoring, and crop-loss attribution caveats. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every pollinator-decline claim is either blocked, softened to proxy-based risk language with explicit monitoring-gap caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match agricultural extension and conservation decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the pollination-service risk analysis would change any real decision for agricultural research institutions, extension services, or conservation programs without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for agricultural and conservation audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that proxy-based risk maps are treated as verified decline measurements or that crop-loss attributions lack direct evidence. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., national agricultural research institute entomologist), named decision (e.g., pollinator habitat conservation targeting), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for pesticide ban justification without direct evidence), and replicated field-reality review.
